text: Fill in the blanks about the orbit and extraocular muscles.
questions: The mnemonic LR6 SO4 rest 3 means the lateral rectus is supplied by CN *VI/6/abducent:sixth cranial nerve*, the superior oblique by CN *IV/4/trochlear:fourth cranial nerve*, and all remaining extraocular muscles by CN III.
Horner's syndrome presents with the triad of *ptosis:drooping eyelid*, *miosis:constricted pupil*, and anhidrosis (loss of sweating) on the affected side.
The common tendinous ring from which the four recti muscles arise is called the *annulus of Zinn/annulus:ring-shaped origin of recti muscles*.

In oculomotor (CN III) palsy, the eye is deviated *down and out/laterally and inferiorly:position due to unopposed lateral rectus and superior oblique* due to the unopposed actions of lateral rectus and superior oblique.

Tears drain from the lacrimal puncta into lacrimal canaliculi, then the lacrimal sac, and finally the *nasolacrimal:duct draining into the inferior meatus of the nose* duct which opens into the inferior meatus of the nose.
